summary: Iteration 40 Testing Complete - All 5 UI improvements VERIFIED working. 1) Pod Management Add Member modal has search and department filter with radio buttons, 2) Access Scopes Assign Employee modal has search and shows role='employee' users, 3) Access Scopes Create/Edit modal has search for Countries and Market Segments, 4) Backend accepts role='employee' for scope assignment (fixed model bug), 5) Employees page has working grid/list view toggle with table view showing correct columns.

minor.issue: Fixed EmployeeScopeAssignmentCreate model - removed scope_id from body since it's passed in URL path. Model at /app/backend/models/employee_scope.py

feature_1_pod_add_member_modal.radio_buttons: PASS - 9 radio buttons found for employee selection (not Select dropdown)

feature_2_access_scopes_assign_modal.shows_employee_role_users: PASS - Users with role='employee' visible in list (e.g., direct_emp_77792@test.com · employee)

feature_3_access_scopes_create_modal.country_search: PASS - data-testid=scope-country-search found and filters countries

feature_4_backend_employee_role.role_check: PASS - Backend line 206 includes 'employee' in role validation
feature_4_backend_employee_role.scope_assignment: PASS - Successfully assigned scope to user with role='employee'

feature_5_employees_grid_list_toggle.list_view: PASS - data-testid=employees-list shows table with columns: Employee, Contact, Department, Pod, Status, Salary, Actions

critical_code_review_comments: All UI features properly implemented with data-testid attributes for testing
PodManagement.jsx correctly uses radio buttons for employee selection instead of Select dropdown
EmployeeScopeManagement.jsx fetches both /users/ and /employees/ to build combined list including role='employee' users
Backend employee_scopes.py line 206 correctly includes 'employee' in role check for scope assignment
seed_data_creation: Used existing test employees with role='employee' for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 40 verified 5 UI improvements: 1) Pod Management Add Member modal now has search input, department filter dropdown, and uses radio buttons for employee selection (not dropdown). 2) Access Scopes Assign Employee modal has search and displays users with role='employee'. 3) Access Scopes Create/Edit modal has search for Countries and Market Segments. 4) Backend /api/employee-scopes/{scope_id}/assign accepts role='employee' users. 5) Employees page has grid/list view toggle - list view shows table with Employee/Contact/Department/Pod/Status/Salary/Actions columns. All features working correctly.
